title: Conditioning Strength and Guidance
concept: The strength of text conditioning combines with classifier-free guidance (callback to Unit 328) to control how closely the output follows the prompt.

exercises.prompt: When you turn up a music volume knob, what happens?

options.text: The sound follows the source more strongly and loudly
The song changes completely into a different song

exercises.explain: Turning up a knob makes the signal stronger — just like turning up 'guidance' makes an AI image follow the prompt more strongly.

cards.title: Turn the Knob!
cards.body: Imagine an AI artist painting your prompt. Do you want it to stick EXACTLY to your words, or explore its own creative ideas a bit? There's a knob for that — and it's called guidance strength!

cards.title: What is Conditioning Strength?
cards.body: Conditioning strength (guided by classifier-free guidance, or CFG) controls how strongly an AI image generator follows your text prompt. A low strength lets the AI wander creatively. A high strength forces it to match your words very closely.

cards.title: The Recipe Chef
cards.body: Think of a chef given a recipe. With low guidance, the chef treats the recipe as a loose suggestion and adds their own flair. With high guidance, the chef follows every single instruction exactly, no substitutions allowed!

cards.title: Prompt: 'A dragon in a teacup'
cards.body: Low guidance (like 2): You might get a fuzzy, dreamy creature loosely near a cup. High guidance (like 12): You get a crisp, clearly recognizable dragon perfectly sitting inside a teacup, matching your words closely.

cards.title: Myth: Higher Is Always Better
cards.body: Myth: Cranking guidance to the max always makes the best picture. Truth: Too much guidance can make images look strange, overly sharp, or distorted. There's usually a sweet spot in the middle!

cards.title: Low vs High Guidance
cards.body: The same prompt can look totally different depending on how strongly the AI is guided to follow it.

left.text: Dreamy, loose, more creative freedom, may drift from the prompt

right.text: Sharp, precise, strictly follows the prompt, less creative freedom

exercises.prompt: What does classifier-free guidance (CFG) strength control?

options.text: How fast the AI generates the image
How closely the output follows the text prompt
The file size of the final image
The color of the AI's logo

exercises.explain: Guidance strength is all about how tightly the AI sticks to your prompt's description.

exercises.prompt: Setting guidance strength extremely high always produces the best possible image.
exercises.explain: False! Extremely high guidance can actually distort or oversharpen images. A balanced middle setting is usually best.

exercises.sentence: A ___ guidance strength makes the AI follow the text prompt more strictly and precisely.
exercises.explain: High guidance strength means the AI sticks very closely to what your prompt describes.

exercises.prompt: Which image was likely generated with LOW guidance strength: a photorealistic cat exactly matching the prompt, or a dreamy, abstract blend of cat-like shapes?

options.text: The dreamy, abstract blend of cat-like shapes
The photorealistic cat exactly matching the prompt
Both were generated with the same guidance strength

exercises.explain: Low guidance lets the AI drift creatively, often producing looser, dreamier results that don't strictly match the prompt.

exercises.prompt: You're using an AI art tool and want the picture to match your detailed prompt as exactly as possible, even if it looks a bit less 'artsy'. What should you do?

options.text: Lower the guidance strength to near zero
Guidance strength has no effect on this

exercises.explain: Increasing guidance strength forces the AI to stick more closely to your prompt's exact description, just like turning up that conditioning knob!
